keyRules: Max. 120 m above ground level (EASA Open category)
Registration as a UAS operator for drones weighing 250 g or more or equipped with a camera
Mandatory Online Training A1/A3 — Minimum Age 16
An 8-km protection zone around airports (larger than the EASA standard)
The areas surrounding Sofia Airport cover a significant portion of the city
Pre-flight coordination with BULATSA is required
Always maintain visual line of sight (VLOS) with the drone
Do not fly over assemblies of people. Rules for uninvolved persons depend on subcategory, class mark and operating conditions; check the official matrix.
Night flight is permitted under the EASA open-category baseline when the required green flashing light is used, unless a current geographic zone or national rule imposes a stricter condition.
National Parks and Nature Reserves: Restrictions Apply
importantNotes: Pre-flight coordination with BULATSA is required before a flight
8-km safety zone around airports — larger than the EASA standard
The areas surrounding Sofia Airport restrict flights over a large part of the city
Military facilities: areas where flying is strictly prohibited
The GDPR applies when photographing recognizable individuals

fields.disclaimerText: Before a flight, always check the official sources of DG CAA and BULATSA. This app is a tool—it is not a legal authority. Bulgaria follows EASA regulations with national additions, including a requirement for pre-flight coordination.
